title: Parser: call_expression vs type_conversion_expression ambiguity (Go + possibly others)
description: After the trailing newline fix, 7 of Go's remaining 12 corpus failures are caused by the parser resolving f(x) as type_conversion_expression instead of call_expression. Examples: println(x) → type_conversion_expression(type_identifier, identifier), time.After(1) → type_conversion_expression(qualified_type, int_literal), c(d) → type_conversion_expression(type_identifier, identifier). Affected tests: Nested_call_expressions, Type_assertion_expressions, Type_conversion_expressions, For_statements, Nested_statement_blocks, Type_switch_statements, Select_statements. The Go grammar uses dynamic precedence to prefer call_expression. Root cause is likely in how we generate or handle precedence values in the grammar generator / parser. Investigation pending.
